title: Semantic Construct Graph (Layer 2)
description: Interpretive, application-level, evidence-backed graph. Every node must have provenance (grounded_in). Stored at .work/semantic-loop/semantic-graph.json.

source_node.description: Id of a node in source-graph.json this evidence refers to; null only when the evidence is a raw artefact not represented as a node.

file.description: Repository-relative path of the evidence. Required for parsed and asserted evidence; observed evidence references a journey instead.

evidence_type.description: What kind of evidence this is, e.g. 'action-mapping', 'handler-method', 'route-definition', 'ddl-statement'.

kind.description: Evidence class. parsed = deterministic parser output over the source tree; observed = recorded from executing the target; asserted = claim extracted from in-repository documentation. Absent means parsed (backward compatibility). Authority order for existence: parsed > observed > asserted; see LOOP.md.

claim_ref.description: For kind 'asserted': id of the claim in doc-claims.json this evidence derives from.

journey_ref.description: For kind 'observed': id of the journey in runtime/journeys.json this evidence derives from. Observed provenance is a replayable-trace reference, not a file/span claim.

trace_ref.description: For kind 'observed': optional work-relative path of the specific trace file within the journey, e.g. 'runtime/traces/login-task-review/step-2.json'.

allOf.$comment: Shape split (RT-1): parsed/asserted evidence cites a repository file; observed evidence cites a journey in runtime/journeys.json.

conflict.description: A recorded disagreement between evidence classes. Conflicts are represented, never silently resolved: both the claim and the counter-evidence stay in the graph.

claim.description: The disputed assertion, e.g. 'docs state managers approve tasks'.

claim_ref.description: Id of the originating claim in doc-claims.json, when the claim came from documentation.

counter_evidence.description: What contradicts the claim and where, e.g. 'no approval action in WEB-INF/struts-config.xml; no handler method found'.

status.description: resolved only when a later iteration found evidence settling the disagreement; the resolution must be stated in counter_evidence or properties.

type.description: A type_id from semantic-types.json. Use UnknownSemanticConstruct when meaning cannot be established.

grounded_in.description: Provenance is mandatory. A semantic node without evidence must not exist; verification fails otherwise.

unknowns.description: Aspects of this construct that could not be determined from local evidence.

conflicts.description: Recorded disagreements between evidence classes affecting this node (e.g. a documentation claim the code contradicts).

allOf.$comment: Authority rule: asserted (documentation) evidence is never sufficient for existence. A node grounded only in asserted evidence may hold candidate/proposed status, or be an UnknownSemanticConstruct — never validated/accepted.

type.description: Application-level relation, e.g. part-of, triggers, renders, reads-from, writes-to, integrates-with, secured-by.

grounded_in.description: Optional edge-level evidence when the relation itself needs proof beyond its endpoints.
